summary: Set 032 Session 1: audit-resolution cycle for the orchestrator check-out / check-in migration. Routed a 5-item audit packet (H1 writer authority, H2 single source of truth, H3 hard vs. advisory, OQ1 field merge, OQ2 events as types or aliases) through Gemini Pro (routed) + GPT-5.4 (429 → manual paste, matching pre-audit pattern). Both engines confirmed all 5 originals. GPT-5.4 raised a NEW H4 (holder identity key); Gemini Pro refined to engine + provider composite over pre-audit's engine-only; operator adjudicated 2026-05-19 = lock composite. Six verdicts locked: H1 router-only writes / hooks become invokers; H2 session-state.json canonical + .dabbler/orchestrator.json RETIRED; H3 hard coordination at write time + explicit operator override (refusal error names holder + release paths); H4 engine+provider composite identity; OQ1 merge into existing orchestrator block + 2 nested fields (checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt); OQ2 work_checked_out / work_checked_in are aliases for work_started / closeout_succeeded with no ledger schema change. Documented in proposal-addendum.md §9 + README.md 'Audit resolution' section. Round-A verification (gemini-pro $0.013) REJECTED on H3 refusal-error-content omission; fix applied; Round-B (gemini-pro $0.019) VERIFIED all 6 items. Cumulative Set 032 Session 1 routed spend: ~$0.044 of $1.00 NTE.

reason.specifics: Session 2 drafts Set 033's implementation spec.md from the 6 locked verdicts (H1, H2, H3, H4, OQ1, OQ2). Work is structured composition: translating verdicts into per-session Steps/Creates/Touches/Ends-with/Progress-keys for a 6-session implementation plan + cross-provider review for sequencing mistakes. Opus 4.7 keeps the spec's internal references coherent (per-session dependencies, file paths, test layers, H4 identity-key rule threading through start_session + multi-set rendering + Playwright tests). Medium effort: design questions are closed, work is execution-style, not heavy design. The cross-provider review (Gemini Pro, cost-capped $0.15) catches sequencing/dependency mistakes before Set 033 starts.
